title: Streaking bacteria on an agar plate from a glycerol stock
author: Cristian Riccio

steps_raw.instruction: Transfer the glycerol stock onto dry ice next to a flame.
Rub the loop in the glycerol stock.
Streak the bacteri in zigzag over half the agar plate.
Sterilise the loop in the flame.
Zigzag over half the plate which overlaps with the first half by a quarter of a plate.
Zigzag over half the plate which overlaps with the second half by a quarter of a plate.
Incube overnight at the temperature which is optimal for bacterial growth, 37 C for E. coli.
The next day, you should have single colonies visible on a quarter of the plate or half the plate.
